claim: Thinking budgets and thought summaries give developers control over reasoning depth in Gemini 2.5 Pro; full thoughts vs. summaries enable different debug/UX tradeoffs
actionable: Use thought summaries for faster iteration and lower token costs; enable full thoughts only when debugging model reasoning or when token budget allows for transparency
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Gemini in 2025 and Realtime Voice AI

claim: Native audio output and multilingual speech in Gemini Live enable seamless language switching without separate TTS models, improving latency and language support
actionable: Use Gemini's native audio output for voice agents needing sub-second latency and multi-language support; avoid composing separate TTS models for better performance
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Gemini in 2025 and Realtime Voice AI

claim: URL context tool paired with search allows building research agents by retrieving in-depth web page information while respecting publisher ecosystems
actionable: Build research agents using Gemini's URL context + search to fetch and summarize web pages; use instead of scraping to maintain publisher relationships
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Gemini in 2025 and Realtime Voice AI

claim: Implicit prompt caching automatically caches repeated context without developer management, reducing costs and latency for chat-on-same-content use cases
actionable: Enable implicit caching for chat applications with persistent context; you save money automatically without managing cache keys or TTLs
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Gemini in 2025 and Realtime Voice AI

claim: Live API session length constraints improved from 15-20 min audio / 5 min video initially; now supports hours-long sessions with sliding windows and resolution controls
actionable: For long-running voice/video agents (customer support, gaming), use sliding window and adaptive resolution to work within Live API session limits
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Gemini in 2025 and Realtime Voice AI

claim: Voice activity detection is challenging at sub-700ms latency; developers can tune sensitivity and disable VAD to bring custom implementations
actionable: When building real-time voice agents, tune Gemini's VAD sensitivity or implement your own if off-the-shelf VAD doesn't fit your use case; latency is the key constraint
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Gemini in 2025 and Realtime Voice AI

claim: Live API requires higher infrastructure commitment than chat completions; bespoke infrastructure per provider makes switching models costly compared to text APIs
actionable: Before committing to real-time voice APIs, ensure long-term confidence in the provider and model roadmap; switching costs are high once you optimize for latency
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Gemini in 2025 and Realtime Voice AI

claim: Asymmetric model splitting (audio in + text search, then audio out) and audio-to-audio architectures each have different latency/quality tradeoffs for voice agents
actionable: Choose audio-in/text-search for complex workflows needing intermediate processing; use audio-to-audio for simple conversations requiring minimal latency
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Gemini in 2025 and Realtime Voice AI

claim: Changing system instructions mid-conversation enables multi-state agents and hand-offs between agents without restarting sessions
actionable: Build multi-step voice workflows by changing system instructions at state transitions (e.g., switching from initial routing agent to specialist agent) within same session
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Gemini in 2025 and Realtime Voice AI

claim: Gemini Diffusion enables generative UI where models generate UI on-the-fly based on user interactions without pre-compiled designs; requires low token latency
actionable: For applications needing dynamic UI (adaptive dashboards, code-generated layouts), Gemini Diffusion enables streaming UI generation with reasoning capabilities
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Gemini in 2025 and Realtime Voice AI

claim: Proactive audio feature prevents agents from responding to irrelevant background audio and enables multi-speaker recognition (speaker diarization) emergently without explicit training
actionable: Use proactive audio to ignore background conversations and enable multi-person voice agents that distinguish between participants without separate speaker ID models
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Gemini in 2025 and Realtime Voice AI
